calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-03-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Petitioner.

The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-12-2011 Continued To Nov-02-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Adminstrator.

The matter was continued to November 2, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Ferdon to file his declaration to support his request for extraordinary attorney's fees.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Whitehead.

At 10:13am, John Mehan appeared in Court after the case was concluded. The Court informed him of the continuance date.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-07-2011 Continued To Oct-12-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-09-2011 Continued To Sep-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-05-2011 Continued To Aug-09-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-17-2011 Continued To Jul-05-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon stated that the hold up is with the request for withhold because of a lien by the Internal Revenue Service and that a release will be filed in six weeks. The matter was continued to July 5, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lazatin.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-22-2011 Continued To May-17-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Guardian.

The matter was continued to May 17, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a response from the Internal Revenue Service regarding the tax issue. The Court informed Mr. Ferdon that the fee request appeared to be extraordinary and that a declaration in support of said fees was required pursuant to CRC 7.702.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Mar-01-2011 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Jan-11-2011 Continued To Mar-01-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon stated that the inventory had been sent to the Referee to be corrected and that they have information regarding all of the Cousins and heirship. The matter was continued to March 1, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Oct-12-2010 Continued To Jan-11-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

The Court stated that the Inventory and Appraisal had been filed and noted that there was no real property in California. Mr. Ferdon stated that the Florida property was appraised, that the Florida Estate is now closed, that some relatives believe that there was a third state involved; Michigan, that these relatives believed that the decendent's parents were well to do in Michigan, that they were shown photos of the property that was left in Michigan, and that they now know that if there were items of value in Michigan that they certainly didn't exist at the time of Ms. Haskill's death. In addition, Mr. Ferdon stated that they would be proceeding with the determination of entitlement and that they will have the two gentlement that contacted the Court file their heirship. The Court stated that Mr. Crane is in his eighties and that he was worried that he couldn't track all the information down. Mr. Ferdon stated that Mr. Crane was able to track down the information with a cousin of his in Florida, that the SFPA now knows who the relatives are, and that they are proceeding with filing their final account. The matter was continued to January 11, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status on the close of the Estate.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate.
Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Continued To Oct-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court stated that Mr. Krainess had been in touch with the Court, that he has indicated who the Heirs are, that the Parties are far flung and that they were unable to get birth certificates. Mr. Ferdon stated that there was an estate in California, Michigan and Florida, that they found nothing in Michigan, that he has asked for documentation but that he received nothing and that he would send everyone a letter requesting affidivates under penalty of perjury and any documents. The matter was continued to October 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of the Inventory and Appraisal, and close of the Estate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-23-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator.\

Mr. Ferdon stated that he called Mr. Krainess, that Mr. Krainess claimed to be a cousin, and that Heir Hunters had found him. In addition, Mr. Ferdon stated that the Decedent had never closed her parent's estate, that the matter was very involved and that Mr. Krainess never called him back. The Court granted the petition appointing the San Francisco Public Administrator, (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Nici Rodich, CSR#11604. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.
